frameworks.name: Launch Then Integrate B2B Partnership Model
frameworks.definition: Enter a B2B partner relationship with a one-time co-webinar launch to build goodwill, then transition to permanent integration into the partner's sales motion so the partner sells the complementary service as a standard add-on.

claims.text: Alex Hormozi advised Will (a virtual-assistant company serving PT/OT/SLP practices doing $7M/year) that the order-of-magnitude growth unlock is learning to run paid webinars via Meta Ads, not finding more in-person conference stages, because Meta and Andromeda ad platforms have become precise enough at audience targeting that a single clear avatar call-out in the creative (e.g., 'If you own an occupational therapy, physical therapy, or speech therapy practice with 5+ locations...') will self-select the right audience and remove the calendar bottleneck imposed by a speaking schedule.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Speaker-dependent lead generation business
Hyper-specific B2B avatar (PT/OT/SLP practice owners with 5+ locations)
Founder already creates content and has existing presentation from in-person stages
claims.mechanism: Meta and Andromeda platforms have become accurate enough at avatar targeting that the qualifying signal is embedded in the ad creative itself (avatar call-out in headline), eliminating the need for calendar-constrained in-person events to pre-filter audience.
bounded_by: Requires an existing in-person presentation that translates to webinar format
Requires ad creative from existing content library

claims.text: Alex Hormozi argued that for B2B service businesses with a hyper-specific avatar, the webinar soft-pitch should end with a free 'savings assessment' (show us your growth rate and current payroll; we'll show you what you could save) rather than a hard close, because the offer frames the VA not as a cost-cutting swap but as a performance upgrade that also costs less, removing the psychological resistance of being sold a downgrade.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: B2B service where the VA (or analogous hire) replaces a higher-cost incumbent
Offer is genuinely better-quality AND lower-cost than the status quo
claims.mechanism: Framing the pitch as 'better than what you have and costs less' removes the buyer's resistance to a trade-down; the savings assessment is a free, low-friction next step that moves prospects into a consultative conversation rather than a close on the call.
bounded_by: Only works when the VA/service objectively outperforms the incumbent on both quality and cost
Does not work as a hard close at the webinar itself

claims.text: Alex Hormozi told Will that a speaker's existing paid-event presentations can be repurposed almost verbatim into webinars with only one material difference: the first few minutes must be crisper because online audiences have lower patience than in-room audiences who are already committed to being present.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Presenter already delivers the same content at in-person paid events
Moving from in-person stage to online webinar format

claims.text: Alex Hormozi recommended that the first 20 to 40 ads for a webinar launch should be built from a founder's highest-performing existing organic content, with a five-second webinar call-to-action appended to each clip, plus a second static-text-overlay version of each ad that does not require a recorded CTA cut-in, because the content already carries the targeting signal and the CTA needs only to redirect intent already established by the content.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Founder has an existing organic content library with top-performing pieces
Launching a webinar as a new acquisition channel

numbers.axis: Number of launch ads built from existing organic content

anchors.value: 20-40 ads (each clip in two versions: CTA cut-in + static text overlay)

bounded_by: Does not apply to founders with no content history

claims.text: Alex Hormozi told Will that when choosing between launching a webinar channel or activating B2B partnership outreach, both strategies will work; the operative decision criterion is which one the founder is more comfortable executing first, because comfort correlates with commitment and follow-through.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Founder has both a content/platform asset (webinar capability) and existing B2B partner relationships
Choosing between two viable channels, not assessing a broken channel

claims.text: Alex Hormozi told Will that B2B partnerships are the first-priority channel over webinars when a founder already has established relationships, because B2B partnerships require no technology setup and leverage pre-existing goodwill that puts the partner 80% of the way to yes before the first pitch conversation.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Founder has existing relationships with potential B2B partners who already made money at founder's events
No existing webinar tech stack or ad account with history
claims.mechanism: Pre-existing goodwill from joint event success means the partner has already experienced value exchange; the sales conversation starts with trust rather than from cold.

numbers.axis: Partner readiness to say yes before pitch conversation

anchors.region: Partner who sponsored founder's event and profited

bounded_by: Does not apply if the founder has no prior relationship with potential B2B partners
Does not apply if the founder is unwilling to push through the belief that integration feels takey

claims.text: Alex Hormozi argued that the long-term goal of a B2B partnership integration is not a one-off co-webinar but embedding the complementary service directly into the partner's standard sales motion, so the partner sells their core product and automatically introduces the VA service as the mechanism that makes the partner's product cost-neutral for the customer.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Partner sells a recurring-revenue service to the same avatar
The complementary service (VAs) can credibly be framed as fully offsetting the partner's fee
claims.mechanism: When the partner can say 'our partner will reduce your payroll costs so that our entire service is effectively free,' the partner's sales team gains a built-in objection handler that removes price friction; this creates a sustained referral stream without additional marketing spend by the VA company.
bounded_by: Requires the VA savings to legitimately exceed the partner's monthly fee
Does not work if the partner's product and the VA service serve different buyer stages

claims.text: Alex Hormozi noted that Will's existing in-person webinar or speaking presentations already carry credibility because attendees at those events paid to be there, and framing the online webinar as 'the same content I gave to paying audiences, now free from your home' creates a perceived bargain that increases opt-in conversion.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Founder has given the same presentation at paid events
Webinar is free to attend
claims.mechanism: The paid-event proof acts as an implicit price anchor; free access to content others paid for triggers a perceived bargain without requiring any credentials, discounts, or testimonials in the ad itself.
bounded_by: Only applicable when the founder genuinely spoke at paid events that charged attendees

claims.text: Alex Hormozi identified Will's growth constraint as a calendar bottleneck, not a market or offer problem: because all leads came from in-person speaking, the maximum annual lead volume was capped by the number of events Will could physically attend, and the event pool was showing declining returns as the same industry conferences were repeated year over year.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Single-channel lead generation (in-person speaking only)
Business at $7M ARR with healthy margins and existing pipeline

claims.text: Alex Hormozi told Will that the VA company's value proposition must be framed as 'a better person for less cost who can also work some of your leads' rather than simply 'the same work for less money,' because buyers only resist cost-cutting proposals that feel like a trade-down; the double benefit (quality upgrade plus cost reduction) removes the primary objection.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Service offering that genuinely exceeds incumbents on quality (offshore VAs vs. domestic staff)
Price is genuinely lower than status quo
claims.mechanism: Buyers resist proposals framed as pure cost cuts because they fear quality degradation. Reframing as 'better AND cheaper' neutralizes the quality objection, leaving no rational basis for resistance.
bounded_by: Requires the service to genuinely outperform incumbents, not merely match them at lower cost

claims.text: Alex Hormozi advised that when a B2B partnership is already in motion from a recent event, the optimal time to pitch deeper integration is immediately after the shared event while goodwill and mutual momentum are at their highest, not in a scheduled follow-up weeks later.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Shared event with partner companies just concluded
Mutual commercial success at the event is freshly visible to both parties

claims.text: Alex Hormozi stated that a healthcare VA business targeting PT/OT/SLP practice owners with 5+ locations exhibits a natural volume-expansion pattern where a single client starts with 2 VAs and scales to 5 and eventually 20 as confidence grows, meaning the unit economics improve non-linearly as client tenure increases.
claims.speaker: Alex Hormozi
conditions: Healthcare VA service with per-VA pricing
Target avatar: PT/OT/SLP practice with 5+ locations

numbers.axis: VAs purchased per client over tenure

bounded_by: Specific to healthcare practice operators who trust the VA quality after first deployment
Dependent on delivering measurable results that justify expansion

examples.text: Will runs a virtual-assistant company serving PT, OT, and SLP practice owners who have 5 or more locations, generating $7M per year. All leads come from in-person speaking at industry events, but the event circuit is starting to saturate — the same conferences repeat and returns are declining. Will has been hiring CEO replacements and raising prices through the Vantage program, improving margins, but the growth ceiling is literally his speaking calendar. Alex Hormozi diagnosed this as a calendar bottleneck and proposed the 10x move: learning to run paid webinars via Meta Ads so the stage is infinite and not constrained by Will's travel schedule.

Will mentioned that he ran one webinar the previous month — one of very few he had done — and it significantly outperformed expectations ('I cleaned up at it'). Alex Hormozi used this as evidence that Will's in-person presentation, given frequently at paid conferences, translates directly to the webinar format with minimal adaptation. The only change required is crisper opening minutes for the online audience.

Will's most recent event had 16 companies sponsoring it, and all of them saw strong results. Alex Hormozi highlighted this as the ideal moment to pitch deeper B2B integration: the sponsor companies already experienced commercial benefit at Will's event, placing them at roughly 80% of the way to a yes before any formal pitch conversation. Hormozi suggested starting with just two partners, pitching integration of the VA service into their sales motion so that their clients could eliminate the partner's service cost through VA-driven payroll savings.

practices.text: Put a banner across the top of every webinar ad that explicitly names the target avatar (e.g., 'Occupational Therapist, Physical Therapist, Speech Therapist') so the target audience self-identifies immediately and non-target viewers self-select out.
For a webinar funnel, deliver a simple opt-in page, send a Zoom link with reminder emails, and keep the logistics friction-free; do not over-engineer the tech stack for an initial launch.

In the webinar content, show 10 distinct use-case examples of painful tasks the target avatar currently does that a VA could handle, rather than walking through the service mechanics step-by-step; outcomes are more compelling than process.

Address the most common VA objections (English fluency, training quality, management overhead, payroll mechanics, employment status) in the second half of the webinar as FAQ rather than waiting for sales calls to handle them individually.

When pushing back on a founder's belief that B2B partnership outreach feels takey, reframe the ask as helping the partner's customers make more money, which enables those customers to spend more with the partner — making the introduction a value-creation event for the partner, not an ask.
